source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An electric dipole consists of point charges \( q_1 = +12 \, \text{nC} \) and \( q_2 = -12 \, \text{nC} \) placed \( 10.0 \, \text{cm} \) apart. Compute the electric potentials at points \( a \).

multi_step: The problem involves an electric dipole with two charges, which typically requires combining contributions from both charges.
To compute potential at point a, the solver likely must first read geometric information from the image and then apply the electric potential formula.
This is more than direct lookup because it combines diagram interpretation with physics computation.

answer_type='short_text', which is easy to compare against an expected numeric result.

registry_passed=true and there are no listed asset issues.
